expectations.text: Response lists at least 3 specific tool names with a 'notion_' prefix
expectations.evidence: Lists 8 tools: notion_search, notion_list_databases, notion_get_database, notion_query_database, notion_get_page, notion_get_page_content, notion_create_page, notion_update_page

expectations.text: Response recommends TypeScript as the implementation language
expectations.evidence: Phase 2 includes npm setup with TypeScript and ts-node, uses TypeScript syntax throughout

expectations.text: Response mentions Zod for input schema validation
expectations.evidence: npm install includes 'zod', and Zod is used in every tool's inputSchema with .describe() chains

expectations.text: Response mentions using an environment variable for the Notion API key
expectations.evidence: process.env.NOTION_API_KEY used in notion-client.ts with startup validation, and shown in Claude Desktop config

expectations.text: Response includes tool annotations (readOnlyHint, destructiveHint, or similar)
expectations.evidence: Every tool has annotations block: read tools have readOnlyHint: true, create/update tools have destructiveHint: false, idempotentHint: false

expectations.text: Response mentions the MCP Inspector or some testing approach
expectations.evidence: Phase 3 shows: npx @modelcontextprotocol/inspector node dist/index.js and a 4-item inspector checklist
